info.title: OpenParlData.ch – API for harmonized Swiss parliamentary data
info.description: ## About This API **Beta Status:** Our API is currently in beta. We welcome your feedback via [GitLab Issues](https://gitlab.com/opendata.ch/openparldatach/data-infrastructure/-/issues) or [by email](mailto:info@openparldata.ch)! **License:** We currently publish our data under the [Creative Commons Licence CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Please attribute as: "Source: OpenParlData.ch" --- ## General API Features ### Pagination **Main Endpoint Pagination:** - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons?offset=0&limit=10 – List first 10 persons **Relation Pagination (Dedicated Endpoints):** - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/1/votings?offset=0&limit=5 – First 5 votings for affair 1 - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons/123/affairs?limit=10&offset=0 – First 10 affairs for person 123 **Relation Pagination (Expand Mode):** - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons/123?expand=affairs&affairs_limit=10&affairs_offset=0 – Person 123 with first 10 affairs - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/1?expand=votings&votings_limit=5&votings_offset=0 – Affair 1 with first 5 votings - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/groups/533?expand=memberships&memberships_limit=20&memberships_offset=0 – Group 533 with first 20 memberships Pagination is supported for all list and relation endpoints using `offset` and `limit` parameters. Navigation links are included in the response. ### Search Functionality Search across parliamentary data with flexible search modes, scopes, and language filtering. #### Basic Search Parameters - **search**: Search query string - **search_mode**: How to match the search term (see details below) - **search_scope**: Where to search (metadata, docs, texts, speeches, or combinations) - **search_language**: Language-specific search (`de`, `fr`, `it`, `rm`, `en`) #### Search Modes (`search_mode`) The API supports four search modes optimized for different use cases: ##### 1. `partial` (Default) **Description**: Case-insensitive substring matching using PostgreSQL ILIKE **Best for**: General searches, user-friendly default behavior **Performance**: ⚡⚡⚡ Fastest (uses pg_trgm trigram indexes) **Examples**: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Budget&search_mode=partial&limit=10 → Finds: "Budget", "Budgetierung", "Haushaltsbudget", "BUDGET report" (~2790 results) - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons/?search=Müller&search_mode=partial&limit=10 → Finds: "Müller", "Hans Müller", "Müller-Schmidt" ##### 2. `exact` **Description**: Case-insensitive exact match only **Best for**: Finding specific terms without variations **Performance**: ⚡⚡⚡ Very fast, most precise **Examples**: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Budget&search_mode=exact&limit=10 → Finds only: "Budget" (exact match, ~61 results) - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons/?search=Philippe Volpi&search_mode=exact → Finds: "Philippe Volpi" (not "Philippe" or "Volpi" separately) ##### 3. `natural` **Description**: Full-text search with natural language processing (PostgreSQL plainto_tsquery) **Best for**: Semantic search with stemming and relevance ranking **Performance**: ⚡⚡ Medium (uses pre-computed tsvector indexes) **Examples**: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Statistik Bundesamt&search_mode=natural&limit=10 → Finds documents with both words in any order (~1755 results with stemming) - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Umwelt Klima Nachhaltigkeit&search_mode=natural&limit=10 → Understands related concepts and word variations ##### 4. `boolean` **Description**: Full-text search with boolean operators (PostgreSQL to_tsquery) **Best for**: Complex queries with precise logical control **Performance**: ⚡⚡ Medium (uses pre-computed tsvector indexes) **Operators**: - `&` - AND (both terms required) - `|` - OR (either term) - `!` - NOT (exclude term) - `<->` - PHRASE (terms adjacent) **Examples**: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Budget & Finanzen&search_mode=boolean&limit=10 → Finds documents containing BOTH "Budget" AND "Finanzen" - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Statistik | Bundesamt&search_mode=boolean&limit=10 → Finds documents with EITHER term - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Umwelt & !Abfall&search_mode=boolean&limit=10 → Finds "Umwelt" but NOT "Abfall" - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=(Klima | Umwelt) & Schweiz&search_mode=boolean&limit=10 → Complex query with grouping #### Performance Comparison | Search Mode | Speed | Results for "Budget" | Best Use Case | |-------------|-------|---------------------|---------------| | `partial` | ⚡⚡⚡ Fastest | ~2790 | General search, user-friendly | | `exact` | ⚡⚡⚡ Fastest | ~61 | Precise matching | | `natural` | ⚡⚡ Medium | ~1755 | Semantic search, phrases | | `boolean` | ⚡⚡ Medium | ~1755 | Advanced logical queries | #### Combining Search Parameters **search_mode + search_scope**: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Klima & Energie&search_mode=boolean&search_scope=metadata,docs&limit=10 → Boolean search in metadata AND documents **search_mode + search_language**: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=Bundesrat Parlament&search_mode=natural&search_language=de&limit=10 → Natural language search in German fields only **All parameters combined**: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?search=(Budget | Finanzen) & Bericht&search_mode=boolean&search_scope=docs&search_language=de&limit=10 → Boolean search in German documents only #### Search Best Practices 1. **Default behavior**: If `search_mode` is omitted, `partial` is used (most user-friendly) 2. **For performance**: Use `partial` or `exact` for interactive searches 3. **For semantics**: Use `natural` for multi-word queries and concept matching 4. **For precision**: Use `boolean` when you need exact logical control (AND/OR/NOT) 5. **Combine with filters**: Always use `limit` parameter for large result sets 6. **Language filtering**: Use `search_language` to improve performance on multilingual data #### Additional Search Examples - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs?search=klima&body_key=CHE&lang=de&sort_by=-begin_date&limit=10 - Search with multiple criteria ### Expand Relations Include related entities in a single request: - **expand**: Comma-separated list of relations to expand Examples: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?expand=docs - Get affairs and related docs - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?expand=events - Get affairs and related events - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?expand=events,docs - Get affairs and related docs and events - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?expand=* - Get affairs and all related entities ### Relations with Pagination Relations endpoints support pagination and navigation links using the following parameters: **Parameters:** - **offset**: Start position (default: 0) - **limit**: Number of items per page (default: 100, max: 1000) - **sort_by**: Field(s) to sort by (e.g. `name`, `date`, prefix with `-` for descending) Navigation links (next_page, prev_page, first_page, last_page) are included in the response. **Examples:** - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons/1/memberships?limit=5&sort_by=id – Memberships with navigation links - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/1/votings?offset=0&limit=2&sort_by=-id – Votings with pagination and sorting ### Field Selection Select specific fields to reduce response size: - **fields**: Comma-separated list of fields to include **Note:** You can also select fields from relations using dot notation, e.g. `memberships.group_name`, `memberships.role_name`, `interests.name`. This works for all entities and relations, including nested relations. Language rules apply as for main entities. Examples: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/bodies/?indexed=true&fields=body_key,legislative_seats - Get Amout of seats per indexed parliament - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons/?fields=body_key,firstname,lastname,party,memberships.group_name,memberships.role_name,memberships.type_external,interests.name&active=true&sort_by=lastname&expand=memberships,interests&limit=100 - Persons with selected fields from relations ### Sorting Sort results by any field: - **sort_by**: Field name(s) to sort by - Prefix with `-` for descending order - Multiple fields: comma-separated Examples: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/meetings?sort_by=-begin_date&limit=10 - Recent meetings sorted by date ### Filtering Filter results using field-specific parameters: - Use any filterable field as query parameter - Multiple values: comma-separated or multiple parameters - Case-insensitive partial matching for text fields Examples: - `?body_key=AI,AR` - Filter by body keys - `?status=active` - Filter by status - `?date_from=2024-01-01&date_to=2024-12-31` - Date range filtering - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?body_key=AI,AR,BS&sort_by=-begin_date - Affairs from AI,AR,BS - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs/?body_key=AI&body_key=AR&body_key=BS&sort_by=-begin_date - Affairs from AI,AR,BS ### Language Support - **lang_format**: - `nested`: Multilingual fields as nested objects (default) - `flat`: Single language fields (as stored in the database) Examples: - `?lang=de&lang_format=flat` - German content in flat format - `?lang=en&lang_fallback=de` - English preferred, German fallback - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs?lang_format=flat&limit=10 - German affairs in flat format ### Data Export Export data in different formats: - **output_format**: - `excel`: Excel file download (.xlsx) - `csv`: CSV file download (.csv) Examples: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons?output_format=excel&limit=100 - Export persons as Excel - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/meetings?output_format=csv&fields=id,name,begin_date - Export meetings as CSV ### Hide Empty Values Clean up responses by hiding null/empty values: - **hide_null**: Set to `true` to omit null values and empty arrays Example: `?hide_null=true` - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/persons?hide_null=true&output_format=csv&fields=name,party,email - Clean export with hidden nulls ### Exclude NULL Values Filter out records with NULL values in specific fields: - **exclude_null**: Field name(s) to check for NULL (comma-separated) - Multiple fields all must be non-NULL (use AND logic) Examples: - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs?exclude_null=begin_date&limit=20 - Affairs with begin_date - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/affairs?exclude_null=begin_date&sort_by=-begin_date&limit=20 - Sort and exclude NULLs - https://api.openparldata.ch/v1/meetings?exclude_null=begin_date,end_date - Meetings with both dates

get.description:
Get overall API usage statistics.

Returns aggregated statistics for the specified time range:
- Total requests
- Success rate
- Average response time
- Error rate
- Number of unique endpoints

**Public endpoint** - No authentication required.
**Rate limit**: 100 requests per hour per IP.
**Cache**: 5 minutes.

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

get.description:
Get top API endpoints by request count.

Returns the most frequently accessed endpoints with statistics:
- Request count
- Average response time
- Error rate

**Public endpoint** - No authentication required.
**Rate limit**: 100 requests per hour per IP.
**Cache**: 5 minutes.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Number of top endpoints to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Number of top endpoints to return

schema.title: Response Get Top Endpoints V1 Analytics Endpoints Get

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

get.description:
Get API usage trends over time.

Returns time-series data showing request volume, response times, and error rates.

**Public endpoint** - No authentication required.
**Rate limit**: 100 requests per hour per IP.
**Cache**: 5 minutes.

schema.title: Response Get Trends V1 Analytics Trends Get

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

get.description:
Get response time performance metrics (percentiles).

Returns:
- P50 (median)
- P95
- P99
- Average
- Maximum

**Public endpoint** - No authentication required.
**Rate limit**: 100 requests per hour per IP.
**Cache**: 5 minutes.

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded
